title: Basic async method
instruction:
An `async` method can use `await`. It returns `Task` (void) or `Task<T>` (value).

Run this.
successMessage: await suspends the method until the Task completes, then continues. The thread is not blocked — it can do other work while waiting.

title: Returning Task<T>
instruction:
Async methods returning a value use `Task<T>`.

Run this.
successMessage: Task<int> wraps the return value. await unwraps it. The async/await pair converts callbacks into sequential-looking code.

title: async void
instruction:
`async void` is for event handlers. Avoid it in other code — exceptions cannot be caught.

Run this.
successMessage: async void fires without waiting. Use it only for event handlers. In other code, return Task so callers can await and catch exceptions.

title: Task.WhenAll — parallel tasks
instruction:
Run multiple async operations in parallel with `Task.WhenAll`.

Run this.
successMessage: Task.WhenAll starts all tasks, then awaits all of them. Total time is the max of individual times, not the sum — real parallelism.

title: Task.WhenAny — first wins
instruction:
`Task.WhenAny` returns when the FIRST task completes.

Run this.
successMessage: WhenAny returns when the fastest task finishes. Useful for timeouts: race a real operation against Task.Delay.

title: CancellationToken
instruction:
Pass a `CancellationToken` to cancel long-running operations.

Run this.

codeChecks.message: Check for cancellation inside the loop.

successMessage: The operation is cancelled after ~35ms. OperationCanceledException signals cancellation. CancellationToken is the cooperative cancellation pattern in .NET.

title: ValueTask for hot paths
instruction:
`ValueTask<T>` avoids heap allocation when the result is often synchronously available.

Run this.
successMessage: ValueTask avoids allocating a Task object on the heap when the result is already available. Use it for high-performance code where the sync path is common.

title: Async streams
instruction:
`IAsyncEnumerable<T>` is async foreach — items arrive over time.

Run this.
successMessage: IAsyncEnumerable combined with await foreach handles streaming data — useful for paginated APIs, live feeds, or large database result sets.
